headlines.title: Voters Head to Polls in Key States, Setting the Stage for Midterms
headlines.description: Voters are gearing up for a key primaries with razor-tight races in six states, including Iowa where Democrats are hoping to win a Senate seat in the traditionally Republican state. Meanwhile in California, former reality star Spencer Pratt is gaining late momentum in his push to lead Los Angeles, the nation’s second-largest city. NBC’s Liz Kreutz reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Housing official who targeted Trump’s enemies is named director of intelligence
headlines.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday named an ally with no background in intelligence to oversee the nation’s spy agencies, taking the helm as the U.S. remains at war with Iran after a fresh round of peace talks stalled.

headlines.title: Israel and Hezbollah trade new attacks despite Trump promise of de-escalation
headlines.description: The Israeli military launched deadly new strikes in Lebanon on Tuesday after accusing Hezbollah of launching attacks overnight, despite President Donald Trump saying both sides had agreed to de-escalate after Iran threatened to pull out of peace talks

headlines.title: New ‘60 Minutes’ boss takes meetings with staffers after clash with Scott Pelley
headlines.description: “60 Minutes” executive producer Nick Bilton pressed ahead with plans to speak individually with staffers a day after correspondent Scott Pelley confronted him.

headlines.title: Protests erupt over U.S. Ebola quarantine center in Kenya
headlines.description: Hundreds of people gathered in Nanyuki, Kenya, to protest a U.S.-proposed Ebola quarantine center for American citizens exposed to the virus. Kenya does not have any suspected Ebola cases, but neighboring Uganda has reported nine and closed its border with the Democratic Republic of Congo.

headlines.title: Meet the Harvard grad who might lead the U.S. to World Cup glory
headlines.description: Matt Freese once wrote a college research project on penalty kicks. Now he’s competing to be the starting goalie for the U.S. men’s national team at this summer’s World Cup.

headlines.title: Coast Guard divers arrive in Bahamas to search for missing American Lynette Hooker
headlines.description: U.S. Coast Guard divers have arrived in the Bahamas as the agency resumes its search for missing American woman Lynette Hooker, who disappeared in April after her husband said she fell from a boat, an official said Tuesday

headlines.title: Los Angeles prosecutors reviewing sex assault allegations against Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs
headlines.description: Los Angeles prosecutors are investigating sexual assault allegations against disgraced artist and music executive Sean “Diddy” Combs, an official said.

headlines.title: Sen. Dan Sullivan accuses Democrats of recruiting Alaska Senate candidate with the same name
headlines.description: Republican Sen. Dan Sullivan’s re-election campaign is accusing one of his opponents in Alaska and Democrats in Washington of recruiting a candidate with the same first and last name: Dan Sullivan.“Mary Peltola and D.C.

headlines.title: Teen rider accused of stabbing three horses at Las Vegas race was ‘stalker,’ injured horse’s owner says
headlines.description: A rodeo rider whose horse was stabbed over the weekend in a Las Vegas barn said Tuesday she came “face-to-face” with the teenager charged with attacking her mare and two other horses.

headlines.title: Playing the waiting game in California: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Steve Kornacki explains why we may need to practice patience with the results in California, which is hosting major contests for governor, House and mayor.

headlines.title: The fish will die regardless: As some Western reservoirs run dry, officials lift fishing limits
headlines.description: Wildlife officials in Colorado and Oregon are allowing people to fish as much as as they want at some reservoirs that are expected to run dry because of drought.
